attractions.name: Chignik
attractions.season: Tustumena ferry calls Jun-Sep; cannery runs Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Salmon cannery village of ~100 on the Alaska Peninsula, a scheduled Tustumena ferry stop with no road anywhere.
attractions.tip: The ferry lays over about two hours - enough to walk the village. The next boat is weeks away.

attractions.name: Sand Point
attractions.season: Year-round town; ferry Jun-Sep, fishing peaks summer
attractions.cost: Ferry from Kodiak ~$200; flight from Anchorage ~$600
attractions.blurb: Aleut fishing town of ~600 on Popof Island, hub of the Shumagins and home to the region's largest fishing fleet.
attractions.tip: Reachable two ways: Tustumena ferry about 8x a year, or a scheduled flight from Anchorage most days.

attractions.name: King Cove
attractions.season: Ferry Jun-Sep; airstrip weathered out often
attractions.cost: Ferry stopover included in AMHS fare
attractions.blurb: Fishing town of ~750 near the Aleutian tip, known for the decades-long fight over a road through Izembek refuge.
attractions.tip: No visitor infrastructure at all - come as a Tustumena stop or with a local contact, not on spec.

attractions.name: Cold Bay Airport
attractions.season: Jet service year-round; brant migration late Sep-Oct
attractions.blurb: One of Alaska's longest runways, a WWII bomber field, and the only practical way in to Izembek's eelgrass lagoons.
attractions.tip: Alaska Airlines jets land here year-round; refuge gravel roads run from the terminal to the Grant Point overlook.

attractions.name: False Pass
attractions.season: Tustumena calls a few times each summer
attractions.cost: Ferry stopover included in AMHS fare
attractions.blurb: Village of ~40 on Isanotski Strait, the narrow gate between the Bering Sea and the Pacific at Unimak Island.
attractions.tip: The ferry transits the strait itself - be on deck, the pass narrows to roughly 1,000 feet.

attractions.name: Shishaldin Volcano
attractions.season: Visible year-round when weather lifts; erupts frequently
attractions.blurb: 9,373-ft near-perfect cone on Unimak Island, the most active volcano in the Aleutians and almost always steaming.
attractions.tip: Best seen from the Anchorage-Dutch Harbor flight; sit on the left side outbound and hope for a clear hour.

attractions.name: Fossil Beach & Narrow Cape
attractions.aka: Pasagshak Road end, Pacific Spaceport Complex
attractions.season: Road open year-round; best Jun-Sep, muddy in spring
attractions.blurb: End of Kodiak's Pasagshak Road: fossil-bearing sea cliffs, a surf break, feral cattle and rocket launch pads.
attractions.tip: 45 mi of gravel from Kodiak, allow 90 min each way; the road closes for occasional Spaceport launches.

attractions.name: Shuyak Island State Park
attractions.season: Cabins year-round; realistic paddling Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: 47,000-acre park at the top of the Kodiak archipelago: sheltered sea-kayak channels and four public-use cabins.
attractions.tip: Floatplane from Kodiak is the only way in; book the cabins months ahead through Alaska State Parks.

attractions.name: Hydaburg Totem Park
attractions.aka: Haida totem park, Prince of Wales Island
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Year-round; carving shed most active in summer
attractions.blurb: Alaska's only Haida community, with a CCC-era park of poles gathered from three abandoned Cordova Bay villages.
attractions.tip: 45 min south of Klawock on paved road; stop at the tribal office first - some poles are actively being replaced.

attractions.name: Thorne Bay & the Prince of Wales road system
attractions.aka: POW logging roads, Coffman Cove, Whale Pass
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Open year-round; logging traffic weekdays
attractions.blurb: Roughly 1,500 mi of logging road link Thorne Bay, Coffman Cove and Whale Pass - the biggest road net in Southeast.
attractions.tip: Mostly gravel, no cell service, few pumps: fill up in Craig or Klawock and carry a full-size spare.

attractions.name: Kake & the Keku Totem Pole
attractions.season: Ferry year-round but thin; most sailings summer
attractions.blurb: Tlingit village on Kupreanof Island with a 128-ft totem pole carved for the 1967 Alaska Purchase centennial.
attractions.tip: The LeConte calls a couple of times a week; the pole is a short uphill walk from the ferry dock.

attractions.name: Angoon
attractions.season: Ferry year-round; paddling Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Only permanent town on Admiralty Island - Tlingit Kootznoowoo, 'fortress of the bears', and gate to Mitchell Bay.
attractions.tip: Start or finish the Admiralty cross-island canoe route here; the Navy shelled the village in 1882 and locals still mark it.

attractions.name: Elfin Cove
attractions.season: Lodges mid-May to mid-Sep; near-empty in winter
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk-only fishing village of about 20 on Cross Sound - no roads, no cars, just planking around the harbor.
attractions.tip: Floatplane from Juneau is ~35 min, but nearly everyone arrives on a multi-day lodge package; day trips are rare.

attractions.name: Chilkat State Park
attractions.aka: Mud Bay Road, Davidson Glacier viewpoint
attractions.season: Road year-round; campground mid-May to Sep
attractions.blurb: Bluff campground at the end of Mud Bay Road with a straight-on view across Chilkat Inlet to Davidson Glacier.
attractions.tip: 7 mi south of Haines; Seduction Point Trail is 6.8 mi one way and needs a low tide at Battery Point.

attractions.name: Treadwell Mine Historic Trail
attractions.season: Year-round; boardwalk icy Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: Ruins of what was once the world's largest gold mine, drowned by a 1917 cave-in; easy 2-mi loop through the forest.
attractions.tip: Park at Sandy Beach on Douglas; the glory hole and the cave-in swimming hole are 10 min in. Free and uncrowded.

attractions.name: Situk River
attractions.season: Steelhead Apr-May; sockeye Jun-Jul; coho Sep-Oct
attractions.blurb: Short, absurdly productive Yakutat river: spring steelhead, then sockeye and one of Alaska's best coho runs.
attractions.tip: Two road accesses - Nine Mile Bridge upper, Situk Landing lower. Float between them in a day with a shuttle.
attractions.duration_note: full day; most people fly in for 3-5

attractions.name: Mount Eyak Ski Area
attractions.season: Weekends roughly Dec-Mar, snow depending
attractions.blurb: Volunteer-run community hill above Cordova spinning the oldest working chairlift in North America, a 1936 Sun Valley single.
attractions.tip: Open only a few days a week - check the hill's social feed the morning you want to ski, not the week before.

attractions.name: Hartney Bay Tide Flats
attractions.aka: Copper River Delta shorebird staging grounds
attractions.season: Peak migration the first two weeks of May
attractions.blurb: Tide flats 5 mi from Cordova where millions of western sandpipers and dunlin stage on the northbound migration.
attractions.tip: Time it to a rising tide, which pushes the flocks in toward the road. Peak is the first week of May.

attractions.name: Copper Center
attractions.season: Year-round; loop road open all seasons
attractions.blurb: Old Valdez Trail roadhouse settlement on the Klutina, the Copper Basin's first supply town for 1898 stampeders.
attractions.tip: The 1932 roadhouse burned in 2012, but the loop off Richardson Mile 101 still has the log church and old cabins.

attractions.name: Gulkana River at Sourdough
attractions.season: Kings mid-Jun to mid-Jul; sockeye Jul; floats Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: Wild and scenic Gulkana - the Copper Basin's king salmon river and a 3-day float from Paxson Lake down to Sourdough.
attractions.tip: Sourdough Creek Campground is the standard take-out; put in at Paxson Lake, Richardson Mile 175, and shuttle a car.

attractions.name: Chisana
attractions.season: Fly-in Jun-Sep; strip unmaintained
attractions.cost: Air taxi from Tok ~$500 round trip
attractions.blurb: Ghost gold camp deep inside Wrangell-St. Elias - a 1913 stampede town of log cabins with no year-round residents left.
attractions.tip: Air taxi from Tok or Nabesna only, and there is no lodging - most people come on a guided fly-in or horse trip.

attractions.name: Petersville Road & Forks Roadhouse
attractions.season: Driveable Jun-Sep; snowmachine route Dec-Mar
attractions.blurb: Gravel spur off the Parks Highway toward old placer diggings; the Forks Roadhouse is the only stop for 30 mi.
attractions.tip: Paved 19 mi from Trapper Creek then rough - 2WD reaches Forks in summer, past that it is ATV country.

attractions.name: Skwentna
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round; Iditarod first weekend of March
attractions.cost: Air charter from Willow ~$300 pp round trip
attractions.blurb: River settlement of about 60 on the Skwentna, the Iditarod's first major checkpoint and a bush-flying and fishing base.
attractions.tip: No road in - skiplane or floatplane from Willow. Race weekend in early March is the one time anything happens here.

attractions.name: Yentna River
attractions.season: Salmon Jun-Aug; Iditarod runs the ice in March
attractions.blurb: 75-mi glacial river feeding the Susitna: the Iditarod's opening run and a string of fly-in king salmon camps.
attractions.tip: Floatplane or riverboat from Deshka Landing. King seasons close some years - check ADF&G emergency orders first.

attractions.name: Nancy Lake State Recreation Area
attractions.season: Paddling late May-Sep; ski and snowmachine Dec-Mar
attractions.blurb: Lake-and-portage canoe country an hour from Wasilla: the Lynx Lake loop links a dozen lakes and 13 paddle-in cabins.
attractions.tip: Cabins book roughly six months out on the state parks site; Tanaina Lake trailhead off Nancy Lake Parkway is the put-in.

attractions.name: Captain Cook State Recreation Area
attractions.aka: End of the Kenai Spur Highway, Stormy Lake
attractions.season: Campground mid-May to Sep; road year-round
attractions.blurb: End of the Kenai Spur Highway: bluff campground over Cook Inlet with Redoubt and Iliamna volcanoes straight across.
attractions.tip: 25 mi past Kenai and nearly empty even in July - the antidote to the Russian River combat-fishing scene.

attractions.name: Fort Yukon
attractions.season: Year-round; midnight sun late Jun, aurora Sep-Apr
attractions.blurb: Largest Gwich'in village, on the Yukon just above the Arctic Circle; holds Alaska's all-time high of 100F, set in 1915.
attractions.tip: A day-trip flight from Fairbanks is the cheapest honest way to cross the Arctic Circle - about an hour each way.

attractions.name: Arctic Village
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round; river season Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Gwich'in village of ~150 on the Chandalar at the south edge of ANWR, center of the fight over the coastal plain.
attractions.tip: Main staging point for ANWR float trips. Visits run through an outfitter or a local invitation, not as a drop-in.

attractions.name: Point Hope
attractions.season: Bowhead whaling Apr-Jun; Qagruq festival in June
attractions.blurb: Inupiaq whaling village on a Chukchi Sea spit - one of the longest continuously occupied sites in North America.
attractions.tip: Spring bowhead whaling and the Qagruq festival are the reason to come; arrange it through the village first.

attractions.name: Gambell, St. Lawrence Island
attractions.season: Birding late May to mid-Jun; brutal the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: Siberian Yupik village 36 mi from Russia - North America's premier spot for Asian vagrant birds and seabird watches.
attractions.tip: Go late May to early June on a package; there is one lodge and you ride ATVs across the gravel to the point.

attractions.name: Little Diomede
attractions.season: Helicopter year-round; ice runway some winters
attractions.blurb: Village clinging to a rock in the Bering Strait; Russia's Big Diomede and tomorrow's date sit 2.4 mi across the water.
attractions.tip: Weekly subsidized helicopter from Nome, weather permitting - budget spare days, people get stranded routinely.

attractions.name: Wales & Cape Prince of Wales
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round; least brutal Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Westernmost community on the North American mainland, at the tip of the Seward Peninsula facing the Bering Strait.
attractions.tip: Small plane from Nome, about 50 min; the cape is a walk from the village. Wind and fog cancel flights constantly.

attractions.name: Aniak
attractions.season: River open Jun-Oct; ice road to villages Jan-Mar
attractions.blurb: Kuskokwim River hub of ~500 that supplies a dozen upriver villages; base for Aniak and Kuskokwim river fishing.
attractions.tip: Daily flights from Bethel and Anchorage. You come for a fishing charter or a village connection, not for sightseeing.

attractions.name: Tok Cutoff
attractions.aka: Alaska Route 1, Tok to Gakona Junction; Mentasta Pass
attractions.season: Open year-round; frost heaves worst in spring
attractions.blurb: 125 mi of Route 1 from Tok to Gakona over Mentasta Pass - the shortcut from the Alaska Highway to Valdez and Anchorage.
attractions.tip: Heaved, lightly travelled, and the access for Nabesna Road and Slana. Fuel is only at Mentasta and Chistochina.

attractions.name: Mount Redoubt
attractions.season: Visible on clear days year-round; clearest Feb-Apr and Sep
attractions.blurb: 10,197-ft active volcano across Cook Inlet from the Kenai; its 1989 and 2009 eruptions grounded Anchorage air traffic.
attractions.tip: Best free view is the Kenai bluff or Captain Cook SRA on a clear evening; Homer air taxis will circle the crater.

attractions.name: Eyak Lake & Power Creek Road
attractions.season: Driveable May-Oct; trumpeter swans on the lake spring and fall
attractions.blurb: Cordova's other road: 7 mi along Eyak Lake to the Power Creek trailhead and the Crater Lake climb above town.
attractions.tip: Gravel past the hatchery and prone to washouts - ask at the ranger district before committing a rental car.

attractions.name: Monashka Bay & Termination Point
attractions.aka: North end of the Kodiak road system
attractions.season: Road year-round; trail muddy Apr-May
attractions.blurb: Where Kodiak's road net ends to the north: pebble beach, spruce forest loop and whale watching off the point.
attractions.tip: About 12 mi from town on Monashka Bay Road; the loop is roughly 5 mi and easy to lose in the moss - carry a track.

attractions.name: Akutan
attractions.season: Ferry a few times each summer; processing plant runs winter and summer
attractions.cost: Ferry stopover included in AMHS fare
attractions.blurb: Aleut village and giant seafood plant on Akutan Island; a Tustumena stop with a hot spring walk out of town.
attractions.tip: Getting off requires the ferry's skiff or a hovercraft from the airport on nearby Akun - not a casual visit.

attractions.name: Atka
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round on a thin schedule; weather rules everything
attractions.blurb: Westernmost permanently inhabited Unangax village, ~50 people, 1,100 mi out the Aleutian chain from Anchorage.
attractions.tip: Flights run a couple of times a week and cancel constantly; there is no hotel - arrange lodging before you fly.

attractions.name: Port Heiden
attractions.season: Flights year-round; Aniakchak trips Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Bristol Bay coast village of ~100 that is the usual overland jumping-off point for Aniakchak's caldera.
attractions.tip: Aniakchak gets ~10 visitors a year; from here it is a 10-mi tundra walk or a bush flight into Surprise Lake.

attractions.name: Savoonga
attractions.aka: St. Lawrence Island; Walrus Capital of the World
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round; walrus hunt and birding late spring
attractions.blurb: Siberian Yupik village on St. Lawrence Island's north shore, known for ivory carving and spring walrus hunting.
attractions.tip: Birders combine it with Gambell on the same package; buying carvings direct supports the carvers, not a gallery.

attractions.name: Shishmaref
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round; sea ice gone Jun-Oct
attractions.blurb: Inupiaq village on a thin Chukchi barrier island that is eroding into the sea and voted to relocate in 2016.
attractions.tip: Half-hour flight from Nome. Come with a purpose and a contact - this is a community, not a climate exhibit.

attractions.name: Teller
attractions.season: Road open roughly Jun-Sep; snowed shut in winter
attractions.blurb: Inupiaq village of ~200 at the end of the 72-mi Teller Road, on a spit between Grantley Harbor and the Bering Sea.
attractions.tip: The drive is the point - musk ox and reindeer along the way. No services in Teller, so fuel and food in Nome.
attractions.duration_note: full day round trip from Nome

attractions.name: Nunivak Island & Mekoryuk
attractions.season: Fly-in year-round; musk ox visible all seasons
attractions.blurb: Bering Sea island with Alaska's founding musk ox herd (transplanted 1935) and the only Cup'ig-speaking village.
attractions.tip: Every musk ox in Alaska descends from this herd; guided musk ox and reindeer hunts are the main visitor traffic.

attractions.name: Unalakleet
attractions.season: King salmon Jun-Jul; Iditarod hits the coast here mid-March
attractions.blurb: Norton Sound village of ~700 where the Iditarod reaches the Bering coast; strong king and silver salmon river.
attractions.tip: The first team to Unalakleet wins a cash prize - mid-March is the one week this town is on the map.
